content:
# Pipeline Redesign: Drop DaVinci Resolve, Sea-thru Color Correction (2026-04-01)

## Decision
Replace the entire Resolve-based color pipeline with Sea-thru physics-based color correction in Python. DaVinci Resolve is no longer a pipeline dependency. User imports batch-processed clips into Resolve (or any NLE) manually if needed.

## What's removed
- Phase 0 (`00_generate_lut.py`) — static LUT replaced by per-pixel Sea-thru correction
- Phase 1b (`01b_estimate_white_balance.py`) — Sea-thru has built-in white balance
- Phase 5 (`05_resolve_setup.py`) — no longer automating Resolve; ffmpeg renders final output
- DRX template, .cube LUT files
- davinci-resolve-mcp server dependency
- DaVinci Resolve Studio purchase requirement

## New pipeline
1. Extract frames (ffmpeg) — existing Phase 1
2. Claude scene analysis — existing Phase 2
3. SAM2 segmentation — existing Phase 3
4. Depth estimation (Depth Anything V2) — existing Phase 4
5. Sea-thru color correction + depth/SAM2 blend — NEW Phase 5
6. ffmpeg render (corrected frames → video) — NEW Phase 6

## Sea-thru approach
- Based on hainh/sea-thru implementation (pure numpy/scipy, CPU)
- Uses Depth Anything V2 relative depth maps from Phase 4 (not metric — acceptable per Teragion results)
- Depth + SAM2 blend mask: near objects get full water removal (in-air look), background keeps blue underwater look
- Cameras: DJI Action 4 (D-Log M) and Insta360 X5 (sRGB flattened)
- C/OpenMP acceleration needed for illuminant estimation at video resolution (from Teragion impl)

## Why
Simplifies pipeline, removes host-machine dependency, removes paid software requirement, enables fully automated batch processing, and Sea-thru produces better color correction than static LUT approach.
